steps.m: to serve, worship, or be devoted to

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form the singular noun for one who serves
Changes singular 'servant' to plural form for a group of servants/worshippers
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنْ (from)
Added نَا at the end to show these are our servants

steps.m: to make something good/right, to fix, to be righteous

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb

steps.m: one who makes things right/righteous person
steps.n: Changes verb into a word describing someone who does this action

steps.m: one who makes things right/righteous person
steps.n: The alif is written as a small mark above the ص instead of a full letter. This is just a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: two righteous ones (describes عَبْدَيْنِ - two servants)
steps.n: Adds ending to show this describes exactly two people. In this ayah, it's describing عَبْدَيْنِ (two servants).

steps.m: to betray trust or be disloyal

steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense form. The و (middle letter) becomes ا when we add the vowels.

steps.m: she betrayed (referring to either the wife of Noah or Lot)
steps.n: Added ت at the end to show a female did the action

steps.m: they both (f) betrayed (referring to both wives)
steps.n: Added ا after ت to show two females did the action together

steps.m: they both (f) betrayed them both (referring to Noah and Lot)
steps.n: Added هُمَا at the end to show the betrayal affected both prophets

steps.m: so they both betrayed them both
steps.n: Added conjunction فَ at the beginning to mean 'so/then'
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it past tense and negative, like saying 'did not' in English
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens as a result of what came before - in this case, after they betrayed their husbands, then they could not help them
Connected root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to make the action causative - doing something to someone else
Changed to present tense by adding يُ prefix and changing vowels

steps.m: that he makes (someone) free from need
steps.n: The َ at the end shows this is in the subjunctive mood, meaning 'that/to' before the verb
This is the basic preposition meaning 'away from' or 'from'
Added هُ at the end to show this means 'him'
Added ما to show this refers to exactly two people (in this case, two righteous servants mentioned in the ayah)
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added connecting alif to link with the next word اللَّهِ (Allah). This is a helper letter that makes the words flow together better when speaking.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Added ال to make it definite and specific, doubled the ل with shadda, and added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by مِن (from) in this ayah
Root letters ش-ي-أ got vowels (َ ْ) to make the common noun 'thing'. This is what's being acted upon in the sentence.
Added ًا at the end to show this is being acted upon. In this ayah, it shows what the two wives could not get from Allah - they couldn't get anything at all to help them.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changing the vowel to kasra makes this a passive verb - something was done to it

steps.m: and it was said (connects to فَخَانَتَاهُمَا - 'then they both betrayed them')
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this phrase to what happened before in the story
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he entered' to a command 'enter!' by dropping the last vowel and changing middle vowel to 'u'

steps.m: both of you enter! (command to two people)
steps.n: Added alif at the end to show the command is for two people
Changes from basic root to noun meaning 'fire'
Added ال at the beginning to say 'the fire' instead of just 'fire'. The ّ (shadda) makes the ن sound stronger.
Added َ (fatha) at the end to show this is what's being entered - when Allah says 'enter' (ادخلا), the fire is what's being entered into.

This is a preposition showing that something happens together with or alongside something else. In this ayah, it shows that the wives of Nuh and Lut will enter the fire along with others who are entering.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to active participle form showing who does the action
Changed to plural form to show multiple people who enter
Added 'the' to make it specific and added final vowel because it comes after مَعَ (with)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is something that happened in the past. In this ayah, Allah is presenting an example.
Added و at the beginning to connect this to what came before. Allah is the one presenting the example.
Added shadda to show that the ل is doubled for emphasis
Changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word, and added damma to show this is the subject doing the action in ضَرَبَ (struck)

steps.m: to be like or similar to something

steps.n: Root letters connected with 'a' vowels (fatha) to make a noun meaning 'example'
Added ًا (tanween) at the end to show this is receiving the action - it's the example being given
This is a relative pronoun that points to someone or something specific. It doesn't come from a root.
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is 'for' or 'to' those people (connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ - who believed)
Added emphasis mark (shadda) on the ل to show strong connection
Added alif after first letter to strengthen the meaning from just trusting to having complete faith
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did this action
Root letters م-ر-أ got vowels (َ and ْ) to make the word for 'person/man'
Added ٱ at start to help pronounce word beginning with sukūn
Changed from 'man' to 'wife' by adding ت at the end - like changing 'he' to 'she'
Added fatha at end to show this is connected to the word after it (Pharaoh)
This is a proper noun borrowed from ancient Egyptian language, meaning the royal palace or great house, which became the title for Egyptian kings
Added fatha at the end to show Pharaoh is being acted upon - he's what she wants to be saved from in 'save me from Pharaoh'
This is a particle that shows something happened at a specific time in the past. Here it introduces what the wife of Pharaoh said at a particular moment.
Added fatha marks to show this happened in the past. The middle letter و changes to ا, which is common in Arabic verbs with و in the middle.

steps.m: she said (referring to امْرَأَتَ فِرْعَوْنَ - the wife of Pharaoh)
steps.n: Added ت at the end to show a woman (Pharaoh's wife) is speaking
Adding fatha and doubling the middle letter creates a noun meaning the one who does this action perfectly
Adding kasra at the end makes this a special way to call out to Allah, like saying 'O my Lord!'
This is incorrect. 'بْنِ' is not the command form - it means 'son of' and is used between names. The command 'build!' would be 'اِبْنِ'.
Adding the imperative prefix 'اِ' to make it a command form
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Added ى at the end to show this means 'me'. This word is connected to the verb ٱبْنِ (build) in this ayah, where the wife of Pharaoh is asking Allah to build for her.
This word is used to show location or possession, like saying something is 'at' or 'with' someone
Added vowel marks (kasra, sukoon, fatha) to the basic letters to make it a usable word meaning 'near/at'
Added كَ at the end to show this is addressing Allah, meaning 'with You'. In this ayah, the wife of Pharaoh is praying to Allah saying 'with You' (in Paradise)
Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
This word is what is being built (the object) in the phrase 'build for me a house in Paradise'
This is a preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it connects 'house' (بَيْتًا) with 'Paradise' (ٱلْجَنَّةِ) to mean 'a house in Paradise'.

steps.m: to cover or hide something completely

steps.n: Changed from basic root to a word meaning 'to cover intensely' by adding vowels and doubling middle letter

steps.m: garden (because it covers with shade and trees)
steps.n: Added ة at the end to make it refer to a specific place that provides cover

steps.m: the garden (Paradise mentioned in this ayah about the wife of Pharaoh)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to specify this particular garden (Paradise) and ِ to show it's connected to the word فِى (in) that comes before it in the ayah
Adding shadda makes the command more urgent/intense
Adds نِى to show who is being saved (me)

steps.m: and save me! (urgently) (part of prayer to اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Adds وَ to connect this request with the previous one in the prayer
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from or originates from something else. In this ayah, it connects to فِرْعَوْنَ (Fir'awn) to show what Asiya wants to be saved from.

steps.m: Pharaoh (the title of ancient Egyptian kings)
steps.n: This is a proper noun borrowed from ancient Egyptian meaning the ruler of Egypt. It doesn't come from an Arabic root.

steps.m: Pharaoh (the object being referred to in this ayah)
steps.n: The fatha (َ) at the end shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence - specifically, it's connected to the word امْرَأَتَ (wife of) in the phrase 'wife of Pharaoh'
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha to form a noun meaning the actual deed or action
Added kasra at the end to show this word is in the genitive case
Added هِ at the end to show possession - 'his work'
The doubled middle letter makes the command stronger, emphasizing the urgency of being saved
Adds first person singular object pronoun to indicate who is being saved
Adding conjunction و with fatha vowel to connect this phrase to previous content
This is a basic preposition that shows something coming away from something else, like saying 'from' in English
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word الْقَوْمِ (the people). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to stand together, unite, rise up

steps.n: The root meaning of standing/uniting together becomes a noun for people who do this action
Adding ال makes it specific (like 'the' in English), and the ِ shows it's connected to مِنَ (from/of)
Changed from the root letters (ظ-ل-م) to past tense verb 'he wronged' by adding the vowels (َ). The root means 'wrong/oppress' and adding these vowels makes it a completed action.
Changes from 'he wronged' (a verb) to 'wrongdoer' (a person) by using the active participle pattern فَاعِل
Makes the word plural to mean multiple wrongdoers
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Makes the word plural and shows it's being used as an object
This is a proper noun of Hebrew origin, so it doesn't have an Arabic root

steps.m: Mary (object of وَصَدَّقَتْ - and she believed)
steps.n: The fatha ending shows Mary is the object being mentioned in relation to the later verb وَصَدَّقَتْ (and she believed)

steps.m: And Mary (object of وَصَدَّقَتْ - and she believed)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect to previous statement

steps.m: to build or establish a family

steps.n: Root 'build/establish' became 'daughter' by adding ta (ت) at end and hamza at start - feminine form of 'son'
Added a fatha mark at the end to connect it to the next word (عِمْرٰنَ), showing 'daughter of Imran'
This is a proper name, specifically referring to the father of Mary (Maryam) who is mentioned in the Quran
Added kasra (ِ) under first letter for proper pronunciation
Added sukun (ْ) on م to show it has no vowel
Changed alif to alif maksura (ٰ) which is a common spelling variation in names

steps.m: Imran (the object being described in this part of the verse)
steps.n: Added fatha (َ) at the end to show this is the object being described in relation to Maryam (Mary) who is mentioned as 'daughter of Imran'
This is the basic form of the feminine relative pronoun used to connect descriptions to feminine nouns (like Maryam in this verse)
Adding ال makes it more specific, pointing to a particular person or thing (in this case, Maryam)
Same word but written in special Quran style - shows it's talking about Maryam
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding 'أ' at the start makes it mean 'to cause/make someone do the action' - like changing 'protect' to 'make someone protected'
Shows this is about a female (Maryam) in the past. This verb connects to فَرْجَهَا (her chastity) which comes right after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to create an opening or space between things

steps.n: Root letters combined with fatha and sukun to make a noun meaning 'private part' - similar to how 'door' comes from 'to open'

steps.m: her private part (object of أَحْصَنَتْ - she guarded)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show it belongs to her (Maryam). This word is what was being guarded in the phrase 'she guarded her private part'.

steps.m: to blow air or breathe into something

steps.n: Connected the letters together and added fatha marks to show this happened in the past
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did this action instead of 'he'. Also added sukun (ْ) before نا because it's needed when adding this ending
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after something else. In this ayah, it shows this happened after Mary guarded her chastity (أَحْصَنَتْ فَرْجَهَا)
Basic preposition showing location or containment. This is a fixed word with no root transformation.
Added kasra vowel mark to show proper pronunciation
Added masculine pronoun ه with kasra to show this means 'in it'. The pronoun refers back to the word فَرْج (private part) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something. Here it connects to رُّوحِنَا (Our spirit) to show where the spirit comes from.
Added kasra (ِ) under م to show how to pronounce it correctly
The root letters were joined together with a damma (ُ) on the first letter to make the word for 'spirit/soul'
The word gets a stronger sound (shadda ّ) because it comes right after مِن, making it more emphasized
Added kasra (ِ) because it comes after the preposition مِن
Added نَا to show this spirit belongs to 'us'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create basic past tense verb
Adding shadda to middle root letter creates Form II intensive meaning
Adds conjunction 'and'

steps.m: and she believed strongly (referring to مَرْيَمَ)
steps.n: Added ت at the end to show the subject is feminine (Maryam)
Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs
Added ة at the end to change from 'speaking' to 'one word' (like adding -ing to walk to get walking)
Added ات to show multiple words
Changed alif to alif maqsurah (ٰ) for writing style
Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by رَبِّهَا (her Lord)
Added بِ at beginning to show 'in/with'
Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to create the noun form meaning 'lord'
Added kasra before the pronoun and feminine possessive pronoun ها to show this is Mary's Lord
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from verb 'wrote' to broken plural noun 'books' by changing middle vowels
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something
Added 'and' at the beginning
Added 'his' at the end to show these are His books
Added fatha vowels to make it past tense. The middle و becomes ا in past tense.
Added ت with sukun to show the subject is feminine (she)
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it. This word is describing Maryam (Mary) from earlier in the verse.
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added fatha at the end to show this connects to the next word ٱلْقٰنِتِينَ (the obedient ones). This is like adding glue to connect two words together.

steps.m: one who is devoted and obedient
steps.n: Added vowel marks and alif to show this describes someone who does the action. This word comes after مِنَ (from/among) in the ayah.
Added ين to make it plural. Since it comes after مِنَ (from/among), it needs to end in ين instead of ون.

steps.m: the devoted and obedient ones (after مِنَ)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific. The ا in ال is written as ٱ when starting a word.
